key_insight: LLMs can help translate algorithm ideas from papers into a working MaxSAT solver, but they still need human oversight and validation.
engineering_relevance: This shows a practical path for bootstrapping solver development from literature, especially for unsatisfiability-based optimization methods and hybrid preprocessing/back-end integrations.
method: Iterative paper discussion with ChatGPT to refine algorithm understanding
Codex-driven implementation of MaxSAT components and configurations
Repeated LLM-assisted code audits, revisions, and benchmarking
evidence_signal: The tested configurations passed fuzzing and MaxSAT Evaluation instances without wrong answers, though performance lagged behind top hand-engineered solvers.
watch_item: Correctness looked good in tested cases, but solver quality and performance still depend on careful benchmarking, external checks, and human guidance.
paper_title: Can LLMs Build a MaxSAT Solver from Papers? The CoreForge Experience
